input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and install latest .NET SDK (includes MAUI workload support)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI version
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui
```

## 3. Install Android SDK Dependencies

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components automatically, but to verify/manage them:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
dotnet workload list
# Typically: ~\AppData\Local\Microsoft\Android\sdk

# Discover installed Android components
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific API levels if needed (discover available ones first)
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;"

# Example: install a specific platform
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35"
```

## 4. Enable Windows App SDK / WinUI Target

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target debugging)
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 5. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (if not using full VS)

```powershell
# Install VS Build Tools with MAUI-relevant workloads
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components via vsconfig or modify:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.XamarinBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet
```

## 6. Accept Android Licenses

```powershell
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 7. Verify the Setup

```powershell
# Check all workloads are healthy
dotnet workload list

# Run the MAUI diagnostic check
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8. Discover Target Frameworks Dynamically

```powershell
# See what TFMs your SDK supports
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed Android manifest versions
dotnet workload list --versions

# Query NuGet for latest MAUI package version
Invoke-RestMethod "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.maui.sdk/index.json" |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty versions | Select-Object -Last 5
```

---

**Key notes:**
- .NET 9 is current stable as of 2026; replace `9` with output of `dotnet --list-sdks` if newer.
- The Windows TFM (`net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0`) minimum may vary — check your `.csproj` after `dotnet new maui`.
- If using full Visual Studio instead of Build Tools, install the **.NET MAUI** workload from the VS Installer UI — it bundles everything above.
